papers.title: Stream-R1: Reliability-Perplexity Aware Reward Distillation for Streaming Video Generation
papers.abstract: Abstract Stream-R1 improves video diffusion model distillation by adaptively weighting supervision based on reliability and perplexity, enhancing visual quality, motion quality, and text alignment without additional computational overhead.  AI-generated summary Distillation-based acceleration has become foundational for making autoregressive streaming video diffusion models practical, with distribution matching distillation (DMD) as the de facto choice. Existing methods, however, train the student to match the teacher's output indiscriminately, treating every rollout, frame, and pixel as equally reliable supervision. We argue that this caps distilled quality, since it overlooks two complementary axes of variance in DMD supervision: Inter-Reliability across student rollouts whose supervision varies in reliability, and Intra-Perplexity across spatial regions and temporal frames that contribute unequally to where quality can still be improved. The objective thus conflates two questions under a uniform weight: whether to learn from each rollout, and where to concentrate optimization within it. To address this, we propose Stream-R1, a Reliability-Perplexity Aware Reward Distillation framework that adaptively reweights the distillation objective at both rollout and spatiotemporal-element levels through a single shared reward-guided mechanism. At the Inter-Reliability level, Stream-R1 rescales each rollout's loss by an exponential of a pretrained video reward score, so that rollouts with reliable supervision dominate optimization. At the Intra-Perplexity level, it back-propagates the same reward model to extract per-pixel gradient saliency, which is factored into spatial and temporal weights that concentrate optimization pressure on regions and frames where refinement yields the largest expected gain. An adaptive balancing mechanism prevents any single quality axis from dominating across visual quality, motion quality, and text alignment. Stream-R1 attains consistent improvements on all three dimensions over distillation baselines on standard streaming video generation benchmarks, without architectural modification or additional inference cost.
affiliations: University of Science and Technology of China

ru.title: Адаптивная дистилляция видео через осознание надёжности и перплексии
ru.desc: Stream-R1 — это метод дистилляции видео-диффузионных моделей, который адаптивно переважает обучающий сигнал на основе надёжности и перплексии. Система работает на двух уровнях: на уровне отдельных прогнозов студента (межнадёжность) переважает потери по оценкам видео-награды, а на уровне пространственно-временных элементов (внутренняя перплексия) концентрирует градиенты на критических регионах и кадрах. Единый механизм, управляемый моделью вознаграждения, одновременно улучшает визуальное качество, качество движения и соответствие тексту без дополнительных вычислительных затрат.

en.title: Adaptive Weighting for Enhanced Video Diffusion Distillation
en.desc: Stream-R1 is a novel framework that enhances video diffusion model distillation by adaptively adjusting the importance of different training signals based on their reliability and perplexity. This approach allows the model to focus on the most reliable rollouts and the most informative spatial and temporal regions, leading to better visual quality, motion quality, and text alignment. By using a shared reward-guided mechanism, Stream-R1 effectively balances the optimization process across these dimensions without increasing computational costs. The results show significant improvements over existing distillation methods, demonstrating the effectiveness of this adaptive weighting strategy.

zh.title: 自适应加权，提升视频生成质量
zh.desc: Stream-R1是一种改进视频扩散模型蒸馏的方法，通过根据可靠性和困惑度自适应加权监督，提升视觉质量、运动质量和文本对齐效果，而无需额外的计算开销。现有方法对学生模型的训练没有区分地匹配教师模型的输出，导致蒸馏质量受限。Stream-R1通过引入可靠性-困惑度感知奖励蒸馏框架，动态调整蒸馏目标的权重，从而优化每个回合和时空元素的学习。该方法在标准流媒体视频生成基准上，在所有三个维度上均实现了显著的改进。

papers.title: Stream-T1: Test-Time Scaling for Streaming Video Generation
papers.abstract: Abstract Stream-T1 enables efficient test-time video generation through streaming synthesis with temporal guidance, achieving improved consistency and quality over existing diffusion-based methods.  AI-generated summary While Test-Time Scaling (TTS) offers a promising direction to enhance video generation without the surging costs of training, current test-time video generation methods based on diffusion models suffer from exorbitant candidate exploration costs and lack temporal guidance. To address these structural bottlenecks, we propose shifting the focus to streaming video generation. We identify that its chunk-level synthesis and few denoising steps are intrinsically suited for TTS, significantly lowering computational overhead while enabling fine-grained temporal control. Driven by this insight, we introduced Stream-T1, a pioneering comprehensive TTS framework exclusively tailored for streaming video generation. Specifically, Stream-T1 is composed of three units: (1) Stream -Scaled Noise Propagation, which actively refines the initial latent noise of the generating chunk using historically proven, high-quality previous chunk noise, effectively establishes temporal dependency and utilizing the historical Gaussian prior to guide the current generation; (2) Stream -Scaled Reward Pruning, which comprehensively evaluates generated candidates to strike an optimal balance between local spatial aesthetics and global temporal coherence by integrating immediate short-term assessments with sliding-window-based long-term evaluations; (3) Stream-Scaled Memory Sinking, which dynamically routes the context evicted from KV-cache into distinct updating pathways guided by the reward feedback, ensuring that previously generated visual information effectively anchors and guides the subsequent video stream. Evaluated on both 5s and 30s comprehensive video benchmarks, Stream-T1 demonstrates profound superiority, significantly improving temporal consistency, motion smoothness, and frame-level visual quality.
affiliations: University of Science and Technology of China

ru.title: Потоковая генерация видео с умным контролем во времени
ru.desc: Stream-T1 — это метод для эффективной генерации видео во время тестирования, использующий потоковый синтез с временным руководством. Основная идея заключается в переходе от дорогостоящих диффузионных моделей к потоковой генерации видео, которая требует меньше вычислительных ресурсов и позволяет лучше контролировать временную консистентность. Система состоит из трёх компонентов: пропагация шума, которая использует предыдущие высококачественные фреймы для создания зависимостей, оценка кандидатов для баланса между локальной эстетикой и глобальной согласованностью, и динамическая маршрутизация контекста через память. На тестах демонстрируется значительное улучшение временной консистентности, плавности движений и визуального качества по сравнению с существующими методами.

en.title: Stream-T1: Revolutionizing Video Generation with Streaming Synthesis
en.desc: The paper presents Stream-T1, a novel framework for efficient test-time video generation that leverages streaming synthesis with temporal guidance. It addresses the limitations of existing diffusion-based methods by reducing computational costs and enhancing temporal coherence. Stream-T1 consists of three key components: noise propagation for establishing temporal dependencies, reward pruning for balancing aesthetics and coherence, and memory sinking for effective context management. The framework shows significant improvements in video quality and consistency across various benchmarks, making it a promising advancement in the field of video generation.

zh.title: 流式视频生成的高效新方法
zh.desc: Stream-T1 是一种高效的测试时视频生成方法，通过流式合成和时间指导来提高生成视频的一致性和质量。与现有的扩散模型相比，Stream-T1 通过分块合成和较少的去噪步骤，显著降低了计算开销，并实现了精细的时间控制。该框架包括三个主要单元：流式噪声传播、流式奖励修剪和流式记忆沉淀，旨在优化生成候选视频的空间美感和时间连贯性。经过评估，Stream-T1 在视频生成的时间一致性、运动平滑性和帧级视觉质量上表现出显著优势。

papers.title: RLDX-1 Technical Report
papers.abstract: Abstract RLDX-1 is a general-purpose robotic policy for dexterous manipulation that integrates heterogeneous modalities through a Multi-Stream Action Transformer architecture, demonstrating superior performance in complex real-world tasks compared to existing vision-language-action models.  AI-generated summary While Vision-Language-Action models (VLAs) have shown remarkable progress toward human-like generalist robotic policies through the versatile intelligence (i.e. broad scene understanding and language-conditioned generalization) inherited from pre-trained Vision-Language Models, they still struggle with complex real-world tasks requiring broader functional capabilities (e.g. motion awareness, memory-aware decision making, and physical sensing). To address this, we introduce RLDX-1, a general-purpose robotic policy for dexterous manipulation built on the Multi-Stream Action Transformer (MSAT), an architecture that unifies these capabilities by integrating heterogeneous modalities through modality-specific streams with cross-modal joint self-attention. RLDX-1 further combines this architecture with system-level design choices, including synthesizing training data for rare manipulation scenarios, learning procedures specialized for human-like manipulation, and inference optimizations for real-time deployment. Through empirical evaluation, we show that RLDX-1 consistently outperforms recent frontier VLAs (e.g. π_{0.5} and GR00T N1.6) across both simulation benchmarks and real-world tasks that require broad functional capabilities beyond general versatility. In particular, RLDX-1 shows superiority in ALLEX humanoid tasks by achieving success rates of 86.8% while π_{0.5} and GR00T N1.6 achieve around 40%, highlighting the ability of RLDX-1 to control a high-DoF humanoid robot under diverse functional demands. Together, these results position RLDX-1 as a promising step toward reliable VLAs for complex, contact-rich, and dynamic real-world dexterous manipulation.

ru.title: Универсальная политика для ловкого манипулирования через мультимодальные трансформеры
ru.desc: RLDX-1 — это универсальная политика для робот-манипулятора, которая объединяет разнородные модальности через архитектуру Multi-Stream Action Transformer. Система интегрирует визуальную информацию, язык и действия, расширяя возможности Vision-Language-Action моделей благодаря специализированным потокам обработки и кросс-модальному самовниманию. Модель дополнена специальными процедурами обучения для манипуляции, синтезом данных для редких сценариев и оптимизациями для развёртывания в реальном времени. RLDX-1 демонстрирует значительное превосходство над существующими VLA моделями в сложных задачах дексторического манипулирования, достигая 86.8% успеха на гуманоидных роботах с высокой степенью свободы.

en.title: RLDX-1: Mastering Dexterous Manipulation with Multi-Stream Action Transformer
en.desc: RLDX-1 is a new robotic policy designed for dexterous manipulation that uses a Multi-Stream Action Transformer architecture. This architecture allows the robot to integrate different types of information, such as visual and language data, to perform complex tasks more effectively. Unlike previous models, RLDX-1 excels in real-world scenarios by incorporating advanced features like motion awareness and memory-aware decision making. Empirical tests show that RLDX-1 significantly outperforms existing models in handling intricate tasks, achieving a success rate of 86.8% in humanoid tasks compared to around 40% for its competitors.

zh.title: RLDX-1：灵巧操作的未来机器人策略
zh.desc: RLDX-1是一种通用的机器人策略，专注于灵巧操作，采用多流动作变换器架构，能够整合不同的感知模态。与现有的视觉-语言-动作模型相比，RLDX-1在复杂的现实任务中表现出更优越的性能。该模型通过跨模态自注意力机制，结合了运动感知、记忆决策和物理感知等多种功能。实验证明，RLDX-1在多种任务中成功率高达86.8%，显著优于其他模型，展示了其在复杂环境下的应用潜力。

papers.title: OpenSearch-VL: An Open Recipe for Frontier Multimodal Search Agents
papers.abstract: Abstract OpenSearch-VL presents an open-source framework for training advanced multimodal search agents using reinforcement learning, featuring specialized data curation, diverse tool environments, and a novel training algorithm that improves performance across multiple benchmarks.  AI-generated summary Deep search has become a crucial capability for frontier multimodal agents, enabling models to solve complex questions through active search, evidence verification, and multi-step reasoning. Despite rapid progress, top-tier multimodal search agents remain difficult to reproduce, largely due to the absence of open high-quality training data, transparent trajectory synthesis pipelines, or detailed training recipes. To this end, we introduce OpenSearch-VL, a fully open-source recipe for training frontier multimodal deep search agents with agentic reinforcement learning. First, we curated a dedicated pipeline to construct high-quality training data through Wikipedia path sampling, fuzzy entity rewriting, and source-anchor visual grounding, which jointly reduce shortcuts and one-step retrieval collapse. Based on this pipeline, we curate two training datasets, SearchVL-SFT-36k for SFT and SearchVL-RL-8k for RL. Besides, we design a diverse tool environment that unifies text search, image search, OCR, cropping, sharpening, super-resolution, and perspective correction, enabling agents to combine active perception with external knowledge acquisition. Finally, we propose a multi-turn fatal-aware GRPO training algorithm that handles cascading tool failures by masking post-failure tokens while preserving useful pre-failure reasoning through one-sided advantage clamping. Built on this recipe, OpenSearch-VL delivers substantial performance gains, with over 10-point average improvements across seven benchmarks, and achieves results comparable to proprietary commercial models on several tasks. We will release all data, code, and models to support open research on multimodal deep search agents.
affiliations: The Chinese University of Hong Kong

ru.title: Открытый рецепт для обучения мультимодальных агентов глубокого поиска
ru.desc: OpenSearch-VL — это открытый фреймворк для обучения многомодальных агентов глубокого поиска с использованием обучения с подкреплением. Авторы разработали специализированный конвейер подготовки высокачественных тренировочных данных через выборку путей в Wikipedia и визуальное заземление, а также спроектировали разнообразное окружение инструментов, объединяющее текстовый и графический поиск с операциями компьютерного зрения. Предложенный алгоритм обучения GRPO умеет обрабатывать каскадные сбои инструментов, сохраняя полезные рассуждения агента до момента ошибки. Модель показывает значительные улучшения более чем на 10 пунктов средне по семи бенчмаркам и сравнима с коммерческими решениями.

en.title: Empowering Multimodal Search with Open-Source Reinforcement Learning
en.desc: OpenSearch-VL is an open-source framework designed to train advanced multimodal search agents using reinforcement learning. It addresses the challenges of reproducing high-performance models by providing high-quality training data and a transparent training process. The framework includes a diverse tool environment that allows agents to perform various tasks like text and image search, enhancing their ability to gather and verify information. Additionally, it introduces a novel training algorithm that improves the agents' resilience to tool failures, leading to significant performance improvements across multiple benchmarks.

zh.title: 开放源代码，提升多模态搜索能力
zh.desc: OpenSearch-VL是一个开源框架，旨在通过强化学习训练先进的多模态搜索代理。该框架提供了专门的数据策划和多样化的工具环境，采用新颖的训练算法，显著提升了多个基准测试的性能。我们构建了高质量的训练数据集，并设计了一个统一的工具环境，使代理能够结合主动感知和外部知识获取。最终，OpenSearch-VL在多个任务上实现了显著的性能提升，结果与商业模型相当，并将所有数据、代码和模型公开，以支持多模态深度搜索代理的开放研究。

papers.title: HERMES++: Toward a Unified Driving World Model for 3D Scene Understanding and Generation
papers.abstract: Abstract HERMES++ combines 3D scene understanding and future geometry prediction through BEV representation, LLM-enhanced queries, temporal linking, and joint geometric optimization for autonomous driving applications.  AI-generated summary Driving world models serve as a pivotal technology for autonomous driving by simulating environmental dynamics. However, existing approaches predominantly focus on future scene generation, often overlooking comprehensive 3D scene understanding. Conversely, while Large Language Models (LLMs) demonstrate impressive reasoning capabilities, they lack the capacity to predict future geometric evolution, creating a significant disparity between semantic interpretation and physical simulation. To bridge this gap, we propose HERMES++, a unified driving world model that integrates 3D scene understanding and future geometry prediction within a single framework. Our approach addresses the distinct requirements of these tasks through synergistic designs. First, a BEV representation consolidates multi-view spatial information into a structure compatible with LLMs. Second, we introduce LLM-enhanced world queries to facilitate knowledge transfer from the understanding branch. Third, a Current-to-Future Link is designed to bridge the temporal gap, conditioning geometric evolution on semantic context. Finally, to enforce structural integrity, we employ a Joint Geometric Optimization strategy that integrates explicit geometric constraints with implicit latent regularization to align internal representations with geometry-aware priors. Extensive evaluations on multiple benchmarks validate the effectiveness of our method. HERMES++ achieves strong performance, outperforming specialist approaches in both future point cloud prediction and 3D scene understanding tasks. The model and code will be publicly released at https://github.com/H-EmbodVis/HERMESV2.
affiliations: Huazhong University of Science and Technology

ru.title: Объединение разума и геометрии: мир автономного вождения через LLM и 3D
ru.desc: HERMES++ представляет собой унифицированную модель мира для автономного вождения, которая интегрирует понимание трёхмерной сцены и предсказание будущей геометрии в единой системе. Модель использует BEV-представление для консолидации информации с нескольких видов камер, совместимое с большими языковыми моделями, что позволяет передавать знания между задачами понимания и предсказания. Временной связи (Current-to-Future Link) обусловливает эволюцию геометрии семантическим контекстом, а совместная геометрическая оптимизация объединяет явные геометрические ограничения с неявной регуляризацией в скрытом пространстве. Экспериментальные результаты демонстрируют, что подход превосходит специализированные методы в задачах предсказания облаков точек и трёхмерного понимания сцены.

en.title: HERMES++: Bridging 3D Understanding and Future Predictions for Smarter Driving
en.desc: HERMES++ is a novel framework that enhances autonomous driving by integrating 3D scene understanding with future geometry prediction. It utilizes a Bird's Eye View (BEV) representation to merge spatial data, making it compatible with Large Language Models (LLMs) for improved reasoning. The model introduces LLM-enhanced queries to transfer knowledge effectively and employs a Current-to-Future Link to connect past and future geometric information. Additionally, a Joint Geometric Optimization strategy ensures that the model maintains structural integrity while aligning its predictions with real-world geometric constraints.

zh.title: HERMES++：自动驾驶的未来几何与三维理解的结合
zh.desc: HERMES++ 是一种结合了三维场景理解和未来几何预测的统一驾驶世界模型，专为自动驾驶应用设计。该模型通过鸟瞰视图（BEV）表示法整合多视角空间信息，并利用增强的大语言模型（LLM）查询来促进知识转移。HERMES++ 还设计了当前到未来的链接，以在语义上下文中调节几何演变，并通过联合几何优化策略确保结构完整性。经过多项基准测试的广泛评估，HERMES++ 在未来点云预测和三维场景理解任务中表现优异，超越了专业方法。

papers.title: PhysForge: Generating Physics-Grounded 3D Assets for Interactive Virtual World
papers.abstract: Abstract PhysForge generates interactive 3D assets by combining visual-language modeling for physical planning with a physics-grounded diffusion model that synthesizes detailed geometry and kinematic parameters through a novel injection mechanism.  AI-generated summary Synthesizing physics-grounded 3D assets is a critical bottleneck for interactive virtual worlds and embodied AI. Existing methods predominantly focus on static geometry, overlooking the functional properties essential for interaction. We propose that interactive asset generation must be rooted in functional logic and hierarchical physics. To bridge this gap, we introduce PhysForge, a decoupled two-stage framework supported by PhysDB, a large-scale dataset of 150,000 assets with four-tier physical annotations. First, a VLM acts as a "physical architect" to plan a "Hierarchical Physical Blueprint" defining material, functional, and kinematic constraints. Second, a physics-grounded diffusion model realizes this blueprint by synthesizing high-fidelity geometry alongside precise kinematic parameters via a novel KineVoxel Injection (KVI) mechanism. Experiments demonstrate that PhysForge produces functionally plausible, simulation-ready assets, providing a robust data engine for interactive 3D content and embodied agents.

ru.title: Автоматическое создание интерактивных 3D объектов через физически обоснованную генерацию
ru.desc: PhysForge — это система для автоматического создания интерактивных 3D объектов, которая использует визуально-языковую модель для планирования физических свойств и диффузионную модель для синтеза геометрии и кинематических параметров. Система работает в два этапа: сначала языковая модель создаёт иерархический план физических ограничений объекта, затем диффузионная модель генерирует детальную геометрию с точными кинематическими параметрами через механизм KineVoxel Injection. Для обучения использован датасет PhysDB с 150 тысячами 3D активов, аннотированными физическими свойствами на четырёх уровнях. Метод производит функционально корректные, готовые к симуляции объекты, которые могут быть использованы для виртуальных миров и воплощённого ИИ.

en.title: Revolutionizing 3D Asset Creation with Physics and Functionality
en.desc: PhysForge is a framework designed to create interactive 3D assets that are essential for virtual environments and embodied AI. It combines visual-language modeling (VLM) with a physics-based diffusion model to generate detailed geometry and kinematic properties. The process involves two stages: first, the VLM creates a 'Hierarchical Physical Blueprint' that outlines the material and functional requirements, and second, the diffusion model uses this blueprint to produce high-quality, simulation-ready assets. This approach addresses the limitations of existing methods by integrating functional logic and physics into the asset generation process.

zh.title: PhysForge：交互式3D资产生成的新纪元
zh.desc: PhysForge 是一个生成交互式 3D 资产的框架，它结合了视觉语言建模和基于物理的扩散模型。该模型通过一种新颖的注入机制，合成详细的几何形状和运动参数。与传统方法不同，PhysForge 强调生成资产时必须考虑功能逻辑和层次物理。实验表明，PhysForge 能够生成功能上合理且适合模拟的资产，为交互式 3D 内容和具身智能体提供了强大的数据引擎。

papers.title: Rethinking Reasoning-Intensive Retrieval: Evaluating and Advancing Retrievers in Agentic Search Systems
papers.abstract: Abstract Researchers introduce BRIGHT-Pro, an expanded expert-annotated benchmark for reasoning-intensive retrieval, and RTriever-Synth, an aspect-decomposed synthetic corpus, to improve retriever performance through agentic search evaluation and LoRA fine-tuning.  AI-generated summary Reasoning-intensive retrieval aims to surface evidence that supports downstream reasoning rather than merely matching topical similarity. This capability is increasingly important for agentic search systems, where retrievers must provide complementary evidence across iterative search and synthesis. However, existing work remains limited on both evaluation and training: benchmarks such as BRIGHT provide narrow gold sets and evaluate retrievers in isolation, while synthetic training corpora often optimize single-passage relevance rather than evidence portfolio construction. We introduce BRIGHT-Pro, an expert-annotated benchmark that expands each query with multi-aspect gold evidence and evaluates retrievers under both static and agentic search protocols. We further construct RTriever-Synth, an aspect-decomposed synthetic corpus that generates complementary positives and positive-conditioned hard negatives, and use it to LoRA fine-tune RTriever-4B from Qwen3-Embedding-4B. Experiments across lexical, general-purpose, and reasoning-intensive retrievers show that aspect-aware and agentic evaluation expose behaviors hidden by standard metrics, while RTriever-4B substantially improves over its base model.

ru.title: Многоаспектный поиск доказательств через синтетические данные и агентивную оценку
ru.desc: Исследователи представляют BRIGHT-Pro, расширенный бенчмарк с экспертной разметкой для оценки поиска с рассуждениями, и RTriever-Synth, синтетический корпус с разложением по аспектам, чтобы улучшить качество рилевер через агентивный поиск и fine-tuning с LoRA. Существующие методы ограничены в оценке и обучении: текущие бенчмарки предоставляют узкие наборы релевантных примеров и оценивают поисковые модели изолированно, а синтетические корпусы часто оптимизируют релевантность отдельных документов вместо построения портфеля доказательств. BRIGHT-Pro позволяет оценивать рилеверы как в статическом режиме, так и при агентивном поиске с несколькими аспектами золотого стандарта. RTriever-Synth генерирует дополняющие друг друга позитивные примеры и условные сложные негативы, что позволяет fine-tuning'у RTriever-4B значительно улучшить базовую модель Qwen3-Embedding-4B.

en.title: Enhancing Retrieval with Multi-Aspect Evidence and Synthetic Training
en.desc: The paper presents BRIGHT-Pro, a new benchmark designed to enhance reasoning-intensive retrieval by providing expert-annotated multi-aspect evidence for queries. It also introduces RTriever-Synth, a synthetic dataset that focuses on generating complementary evidence and challenging negative examples for training. This approach aims to improve the performance of retrievers in agentic search systems, which require more than just topical relevance. The results demonstrate that using aspect-aware evaluations and fine-tuning with RTriever-Synth significantly boosts the capabilities of retrievers compared to traditional methods.

zh.title: 提升推理检索性能的新基准与合成语料库
zh.desc: 研究人员提出了BRIGHT-Pro，这是一个扩展的专家注释基准，用于推理密集型检索，并且构建了RTriever-Synth，这是一个分解了方面的合成语料库，旨在通过代理搜索评估和LoRA微调来提高检索器的性能。推理密集型检索的目标是提供支持下游推理的证据，而不仅仅是匹配主题相似性。这种能力在代理搜索系统中越来越重要，因为检索器必须在迭代搜索和综合中提供互补证据。我们通过BRIGHT-Pro和RTriever-Synth的引入，解决了现有工作在评估和训练方面的局限性，显著提升了检索器的表现。

papers.title: D-OPSD: On-Policy Self-Distillation for Continuously Tuning Step-Distilled Diffusion Models
papers.abstract: Abstract A new training approach called D-OPSD enables efficient supervised fine-tuning for diffusion models by leveraging on-policy self-distillation with text and multimodal features while preserving few-step inference capabilities.  AI-generated summary The landscape of high-performance image generation models is currently shifting from the inefficient multi-step ones to the efficient few-step counterparts (e.g, Z-Image-Turbo and FLUX.2-klein). However, these models present significant challenges for directly continuous supervised fine-tuning. For example, applying the commonly used fine-tuning technique would compromises their inherent few-step inference capability. To address this, we propose D-OPSD, a novel training paradigm for step-distilled diffusion models that enables on-policy learning during supervised fine-tuning. We first find that the modern diffusion model where the LLM/VLM serves as the encoder can inherit its encoder's in-context capabilities. This enables us to make the training as an on-policy self-distillation process. Specifically, during training, we make the model acts as both the teacher and the student with different contexts, where the student is conditioned only on the text feature, while the teacher is conditioned on the multimodal feature of both the text prompt and the target image. Training minimizes the two predicted distributions over the student's own roll-outs. By optimized on the model's own trajectory and under it's own supervision, D-OPSD enables the model to learn new concept, style, etc. without sacrificing the original few-step capacity.
affiliations: The Chinese University of Hong Kong
The Hong Kong University of Science and Technology

ru.title: Быстрое дообучение дифузионных моделей через самодистилляцию
ru.desc: В работе предложен метод D-OPSD для эффективной дообучки дифузионных моделей, которые работают за несколько шагов. Подход использует самодистилляцию «on-policy», где модель одновременно выступает учителем и студентом с разными контекстами - студент учится на текстовых признаках, а учитель использует мультимодальные признаки текста и изображения. Ключевое достижение заключается в том, что дифузионные модели с LLM/VLM-кодировщиком могут наследовать способности работать с контекстом, позволяя проводить обучение как процесс самодистилляции. Метод позволяет модели изучать новые концепции и стили без потери исходной способности быстрого вывода за несколько шагов.

en.title: Efficient Fine-Tuning for Diffusion Models with D-OPSD
en.desc: The paper introduces D-OPSD, a new training method for fine-tuning diffusion models efficiently while maintaining their few-step inference capabilities. This approach utilizes on-policy self-distillation, allowing the model to learn from its own predictions in a supervised manner. By leveraging both text and multimodal features, the model acts as both teacher and student during training, enhancing its ability to learn new concepts and styles. D-OPSD addresses the challenges of traditional fine-tuning methods that compromise the model's efficiency in generating images.

zh.title: D-OPSD：高效微调扩散模型的新方法
zh.desc: D-OPSD是一种新的训练方法，旨在通过利用在线自蒸馏和多模态特征，实现对扩散模型的高效监督微调。该方法解决了传统微调技术在保持少步推理能力方面的挑战，使得模型在训练过程中能够同时充当教师和学生。通过这种方式，模型可以在不同的上下文中进行学习，从而在不牺牲原有少步能力的情况下，掌握新的概念和风格。D-OPSD的提出为高性能图像生成模型的训练提供了新的思路。

papers.title: Lightning Unified Video Editing via In-Context Sparse Attention
papers.abstract: Abstract In-context sparse attention framework enables efficient video editing with reduced computational costs while maintaining visual quality.  AI-generated summary Video editing has evolved toward In-Context Learning (ICL) paradigms, yet the resulting quadratic attention costs create a critical computational bottleneck. In this work, we propose In-context Sparse Attention (ISA), the first near-lossless empirical sparse framework tailored for ICL video editing. Our design is grounded in two key insights: first, context tokens exhibit significantly lower saliency than source tokens; second, we theoretically prove and empirically validate that Query sharpness correlates with approximation error. Motivated by these findings, ISA implements an efficient pre-selection strategy to prune redundant context, followed by a dynamic query grouping mechanism that routes high-error queries to full attention and low-error ones to a computationally efficient 0-th order Taylor sparse attention. Furthermore, we build \texttt{LIVEditor} , a novel lightning video editing model via ISA and a proposed video-editing data pipeline that curated a 1.7M high-quality dataset. Extensive experiments demonstrate that LIVEditor achieves a sim60% reduction in attention-module latency while surpassing state-of-the-art methods across EditVerseBench, IVE-Bench, and VIE-Bench, delivering near-lossless acceleration without compromising visual fidelity.
affiliations: Hong Kong University of Science and Technology (GZ), Hong Kong

ru.title: Разреженное внимание для скоростного видеоредактирования без потери качества
ru.desc: В работе предложен In-context Sparse Attention (ISA) — первый практический фреймворк разреженного внимания для видеоредактирования с обучением в контексте. Авторы показали, что токены контекста имеют низкую значимость по сравнению с исходными токенами, и доказали теоретически, что резкость запроса коррелирует с ошибкой аппроксимации. На основе этих insights разработан механизм отбора контекста и динамической группировки запросов, маршрутизирующий сложные запросы на полное внимание, а простые — на эффективное разреженное внимание. Результаты показывают 60% снижение задержки модуля внимания при сохранении качества изображений на бенчмарках EditVerseBench, IVE-Bench и VIE-Bench.

en.title: Efficient Video Editing with In-Context Sparse Attention
en.desc: This paper introduces the In-context Sparse Attention (ISA) framework, which enhances video editing efficiency by reducing computational costs while preserving visual quality. The authors identify that context tokens are less important than source tokens, leading to a novel approach that prunes unnecessary context and optimizes query processing. By implementing a dynamic query grouping mechanism, ISA directs complex queries to full attention while simplifying others with a Taylor sparse attention method. The resulting model, LIVEditor, demonstrates a significant reduction in latency and outperforms existing methods on multiple benchmarks, achieving fast and high-quality video editing.

zh.title: 高效视频编辑的新方法：稀疏注意力框架
zh.desc: 本文提出了一种名为In-context Sparse Attention（ISA）的新框架，旨在提高视频编辑的效率，同时降低计算成本。该框架通过预选择策略去除冗余上下文，并采用动态查询分组机制，以优化注意力计算。研究表明，上下文标记的显著性远低于源标记，且查询的清晰度与近似误差相关。实验结果显示，LIVEditor在保持视觉质量的同时，注意力模块延迟减少了60%，超越了现有的最先进方法。

papers.title: Awaking Spatial Intelligence in Unified Multimodal Understanding and Generation
papers.abstract: Abstract JoyAI-Image integrates a spatially enhanced MLLM with MMDiT to achieve unified visual understanding, text-to-image generation, and instruction-guided image editing with enhanced spatial intelligence.  AI-generated summary We present JoyAI-Image, a unified multimodal foundation model for visual understanding, text-to-image generation, and instruction-guided image editing. JoyAI-Image couples a spatially enhanced Multimodal Large Language Model (MLLM) with a Multimodal Diffusion Transformer (MMDiT), allowing perception and generation to interact through a shared multimodal interface. Around this architecture, we build a scalable training recipe that combines unified instruction tuning, long-text rendering supervision, spatially grounded data, and both general and spatial editing signals. This design gives the model broad multimodal capability while strengthening geometry-aware reasoning and controllable visual synthesis. Experiments across understanding, generation, long-text rendering, and editing benchmarks show that JoyAI-Image achieves state-of-the-art or highly competitive performance. More importantly, the bidirectional loop between enhanced understanding, controllable spatial editing, and novel-view-assisted reasoning enables the model to move beyond general visual competence toward stronger spatial intelligence. These results suggest a promising path for unified visual models in downstream applications such as vision-language-action systems and world models.

ru.title: Единая модель для понимания, создания и редактирования визуального контента с пространственным интеллектом
ru.desc: JoyAI-Image представляет собой объединённую мультимодальную модель, которая интегрирует улучшенный MLLM с MMDiT для выполнения задач понимания изображений, генерации текста в изображение и редактирования с инструкциями. Архитектура позволяет процессам восприятия и генерации взаимодействовать через общий мультимодальный интерфейс, используя пространственную информацию для улучшения качества. Модель обучается на комбинированном наборе данных с инструкциями, долгим текстовым контролем, пространственно обоснованными примерами и сигналами редактирования, что даёт ей широкие мультимодальные возможности. Результаты показывают, что двусторонний цикл между понимаем, пространственным редактированием и рассуждением создаёт более сильный пространственный интеллект модели.

en.title: Unified Visual Intelligence with JoyAI-Image
en.desc: JoyAI-Image is a cutting-edge multimodal foundation model that combines visual understanding, text-to-image generation, and instruction-guided image editing. It integrates a spatially enhanced Multimodal Large Language Model (MLLM) with a Multimodal Diffusion Transformer (MMDiT), facilitating interaction between perception and generation through a unified interface. The model is trained using a comprehensive approach that includes instruction tuning and spatially grounded data, enhancing its ability to reason about geometry and perform controllable visual synthesis. Experimental results demonstrate that JoyAI-Image achieves top-tier performance across various benchmarks, indicating its potential for advanced applications in vision-language-action systems.

zh.title: 统一多模态模型，提升空间智能
zh.desc: JoyAI-Image 是一个统一的多模态基础模型，旨在实现视觉理解、文本到图像生成和基于指令的图像编辑。它结合了空间增强的多模态大语言模型（MLLM）和多模态扩散变换器（MMDiT），使得感知和生成可以通过共享的多模态接口进行交互。该模型通过统一的指令调优、长文本渲染监督和空间数据的结合，增强了几何感知推理和可控的视觉合成能力。实验结果表明，JoyAI-Image 在多个基准测试中表现出色，展示了其在视觉语言行动系统和世界模型等下游应用中的潜力。

papers.title: MiniCPM-o 4.5: Towards Real-Time Full-Duplex Omni-Modal Interaction
papers.abstract: Abstract MiniCPM-o 4.5 enables real-time full-duplex multimodal interaction through Omni-Flow, a unified streaming framework that aligns inputs and outputs temporally for simultaneous perception and response.  AI-generated summary Recent progress in multimodal large language models (MLLMs) has brought AI capabilities from static offline data processing to real-time streaming interaction, yet they still remain far from human-level multimodal interaction. The key bottlenecks are no longer modality coverage or latency alone, but the interaction paradigm itself. First, perception and response are still separated into alternating phases, preventing models from incorporating new inputs for timely adjustment during generation. Second, most current models remain reactive, responding only to explicit user requests instead of acting proactively in the evolving multimodal environment. We present MiniCPM-o 4.5, our latest effort towards human-like multimodal interaction, which mitigates these gaps by real-time full-duplex omni-modal interaction. It can see, listen, and speak simultaneously in real-time, while also exhibiting proactive behaviors such as issuing reminders or comments based on its continuous understanding of the live scene. The key technique behind MiniCPM-o 4.5 is Omni-Flow, a unified streaming framework that aligns omni-modal inputs and outputs along a shared temporal axis. This formulation converts conventional turn-based interaction into a full-duplex, time-aligned process, enabling simultaneous perception and response and allowing proactive behavior to arise within the same framework. With a total of 9B parameters, MiniCPM-o 4.5 approaches Gemini 2.5 Flash in vision-language capabilities, delivering state-of-the-art open-source performance at its scale. It also surpasses Qwen3-Omni-30B-A3B in omni-modal understanding and delivers better speech generation, with significantly higher computation efficiency. Driven by its efficient architecture design and inference optimization, the model can perform real-time full-duplex omni-modal interaction on edge devices with less than 12GB RAM cost.

ru.title: Полнодуплексное мультимодальное взаимодействие в реальном времени
ru.desc: MiniCPM-o 4.5 представляет собой мультимодальную языковую модель, которая реализует одновременное восприятие и генерацию ответов в реальном времени через новый фреймворк Omni-Flow. Ключевое инновационное решение заключается в выравнивании входных и выходных потоков данных (зрение, слух, речь) по общей временной оси, что позволяет модели обрабатывать информацию параллельно вместо традиционного чередующегося взаимодействия. Модель с 9 миллиардами параметров демонстрирует способность к проактивному поведению, включая самостоятельные комментарии и напоминания на основе анализа окружающей среды. Архитектура оптимизирована для работы на граничных устройствах с потреблением менее 12 ГБ оперативной памяти, обеспечивая производительность на уровне современных систем при высокой эффективности.

en.title: Real-Time Multimodal Interaction Redefined
en.desc: The paper introduces MiniCPM-o 4.5, a model designed for real-time multimodal interaction that allows simultaneous perception and response through a framework called Omni-Flow. This model addresses limitations in current multimodal large language models (MLLMs) by enabling full-duplex communication, where the model can see, listen, and speak at the same time. Unlike traditional models that react only to user prompts, MiniCPM-o 4.5 can proactively engage with its environment, providing timely responses and insights. With 9 billion parameters, it achieves high performance in vision-language tasks while being efficient enough to run on edge devices with limited resources.

zh.title: 实时全双工多模态交互的未来
zh.desc: MiniCPM-o 4.5 是一种新型的多模态大语言模型，能够实现实时的全双工交互。它通过 Omni-Flow 框架，将输入和输出在时间上对齐，从而实现同时感知和响应。与传统模型不同，MiniCPM-o 4.5 能够主动地根据实时场景进行反应，而不仅仅是被动响应用户请求。该模型在视觉和语言能力上接近最先进的技术，且在边缘设备上以较低的内存消耗实现高效的多模态交互。

papers.title: Parameter-Efficient Multi-View Proficiency Estimation: From Discriminative Classification to Generative Feedback
papers.abstract: Abstract Three methods for multi-view proficiency estimation—SkillFormer, PATS, and ProfVLM—achieve state-of-the-art accuracy on Ego-Exo4D with reduced parameters and training epochs while enabling interpretable feedback generation.  AI-generated summary Estimating how well a person performs an action, rather than which action is performed, is central to coaching, rehabilitation, and talent identification. This task is challenging because proficiency is encoded in subtle differences in timing, balance, body mechanics, and execution, often distributed across multiple views and short temporal events. We discuss three recent contributions to multi-view proficiency estimation on Ego-Exo4D. SkillFormer introduces a parameter-efficient discriminative architecture for selective multi-view fusion; PATS improves temporal sampling by preserving locally dense excerpts of fundamental movements; and ProfVLM reformulates proficiency estimation as conditional language generation, producing both a proficiency label and expert-style feedback through a gated cross-view projector and a compact language backbone. Together, these methods achieve state-of-the-art accuracy on Ego-Exo4D with up to 20x fewer trainable parameters and up to 3x fewer training epochs than video-transformer baselines, while moving from closed-set classification toward interpretable feedback generation. These results highlight a shift toward efficient, multi-view systems that combine selective fusion, proficiency-aware sampling, and actionable generative feedback.
affiliations: Faculty of Engineering, Free University of Bozen-Bolzano, Bozen-Bolzano, Italy

ru.title: Эффективная многоракурсная оценка мастерства с интерпретируемой обратной связью
ru.desc: В статье представлены три метода для оценки мастерства выполнения действий на основе видео с нескольких ракурсов: SkillFormer использует эффективную архитектуру для селективного слияния представлений, PATS улучшает временную дискретизацию через сохранение плотных фрагментов фундаментальных движений, а ProfVLM переформулирует задачу как условную генерацию текста. Все три метода достигают state-of-the-art результатов на датасете Ego-Exo4D с значительно меньшим количеством параметров и эпох обучения по сравнению с видео-трансформер базлайнами, при этом генерируя интерпретируемую обратную связь в стиле эксперта вместо простой классификации.

en.title: Revolutionizing Action Proficiency Estimation with Multi-View Insights
en.desc: This paper presents three innovative methods for estimating proficiency in actions from multiple viewpoints: SkillFormer, PATS, and ProfVLM. These methods enhance accuracy while significantly reducing the number of parameters and training time required. SkillFormer focuses on efficient multi-view fusion, PATS optimizes the sampling of movement data, and ProfVLM uses language generation to provide feedback alongside proficiency labels. Collectively, they represent a shift towards more efficient and interpretable systems in the field of action proficiency estimation.

zh.title: 高效多视角能力评估的新方法
zh.desc: 本文介绍了三种多视角能力评估方法——SkillFormer、PATS和ProfVLM。这些方法在Ego-Exo4D数据集上实现了最先进的准确性，同时减少了参数和训练周期。能力评估的核心在于分析动作执行的细微差别，这些差别通常分布在多个视角和短时间事件中。通过选择性融合、能力感知采样和可操作的生成反馈，这些方法推动了高效的多视角系统的发展。

papers.title: ResRL: Boosting LLM Reasoning via Negative Sample Projection Residual Reinforcement Learning
papers.abstract: Abstract ResRL improves LLM reasoning by decoupling semantic distributions between positive and negative responses through negative sample projection, maintaining diversity while outperforming existing methods on multiple benchmarks.  AI-generated summary Reinforcement Learning with Verifiable Rewards (RLVR) enhances reasoning of Large Language Models (LLMs) but usually exhibits limited generation diversity due to the over-incentivization of positive rewards. Although methods like Negative Sample Reinforcement (NSR) mitigate this issue by upweighting penalty from negative samples, they may suppress the semantic distributions shared between positive and negative responses. To boost reasoning ability without losing diversity, this paper proposes negative sample projection Residual Reinforcement Learning (ResRL) that decouples similar semantic distributions among positive and negative responses. We theoretically link Lazy Likelihood Displacement (LLD) to negative-positive head-gradient interference and derive a single-forward proxy that upper-bounds representation alignment to guide conservative advantage reweighting. ResRL then projects negative-token hidden representations onto an SVD-based low-rank positive subspace and uses projection residuals to modulate negative gradients, improving reasoning while preserving diversity and outperforming strong baselines on average across twelve benchmarks spanning Mathematics, Code, Agent Tasks, and Function Calling. Notably, ResRL surpasses NSR on mathematical reasoning by 9.4\% in Avg@16 and 7.0\% in Pass@128. Code is available at https://github.com/1229095296/ResRL.git.
affiliations: MAIS&NLPR, Institute of Automation, Chinese Academy of Sciences, Beijing, China
School of Advanced Interdisciplinary Sciences, University of Chinese Academy of Sciences, Beijing, China

ru.title: Разделение семантик позитивных и негативных примеров для улучшения рассуждений в больших языковых моделях
ru.desc: ResRL улучшает способность больших языковых моделей к рассуждению, применяя усиленное обучение с проверяемыми вознаграждениями. Основная идея заключается в разделении семантических распределений между позитивными и негативными ответами через проекцию негативных примеров на подпространство позитивных. Авторы теоретически связывают явление ленивого смещения правдоподобия с градиентной интерференцией и предлагают консервативную переработку весов преимуществ. Метод сохраняет разнообразие генерируемых ответов при одновременном повышении качества рассуждений на множестве контрольных наборов.

en.title: Enhancing LLM Reasoning with ResRL: Balancing Diversity and Performance
en.desc: This paper introduces Residual Reinforcement Learning (ResRL), a method that enhances the reasoning capabilities of Large Language Models (LLMs) by effectively managing the relationship between positive and negative responses. By using negative sample projection, ResRL maintains diversity in generated outputs while improving performance on various benchmarks. The approach decouples the semantic distributions of positive and negative responses, allowing for better reasoning without sacrificing the variety of generated content. The results show that ResRL outperforms existing methods, particularly in mathematical reasoning tasks, demonstrating its effectiveness in balancing reward incentives.

zh.title: 提升推理能力，保持多样性！
zh.desc: 本文提出了一种新的强化学习方法，称为残差强化学习（ResRL），旨在提高大型语言模型（LLM）的推理能力。通过对负样本进行投影，ResRL能够将正负响应之间的语义分布解耦，从而在保持多样性的同时，超越现有方法的表现。该方法通过理论分析将懒惰似然位移（LLD）与正负头梯度干扰联系起来，并利用单次前向代理来指导保守的优势重加权。实验结果表明，ResRL在多个基准测试中表现优异，尤其在数学推理方面比负样本强化（NSR）提高了9.4%。

papers.title: SWE-WebDevBench: Evaluating Coding Agent Application Platforms as Virtual Software Agencies
papers.abstract: Abstract A comprehensive evaluation framework called SWE-WebDev Bench is presented to assess AI-powered application development platforms across multiple dimensions including requirement understanding, architectural decision-making, code quality, and production readiness.  AI-generated summary The emergence of "vibe coding" platforms, where users describe applications in natural language and AI agents autonomously generate full-stack software, has created a need for rigorous evaluation beyond code-level benchmarks. In order to assess them as virtual software development agencies on understanding business requirements, making architectural decisions, writing production code, handling iterative modifications, and maintaining business readiness, we introduce SWE-WebDev Bench, a 68-metric evaluation framework spanning 25 primary and 43 diagnostic metrics across seven groups, organized along three dimensions: Interaction Mode (App Creation Request (ACR) vs. App Modification Request (AMR)), Agency Angle (Product Manager (PM), Engineering, Ops), and Complexity Tier (T4 multi-role SaaS, T5 AI-native).   Our evaluation (six platforms, three domains, 18 evaluation cells) reveals four recurring shortcomings in the current generation of AI app builders: (1) A specification bottleneck, where platforms compress rich business requirements into oversimplified technical plans, (2) A pervasive frontend-backend decoupling, where visually polished UIs mask absent or broken backend infrastructure, (3) A steep production-readiness cliff, where no platform scores above 60% on engineering quality and post-generation human effort varies substantially across platforms and (4) Widespread security and infrastructure failures, with no platform exceeding 65% Security Score against a 90% target and concurrency handling as low as 6%. These observations are descriptive of our sample and require larger-scale replication to establish generality. We release SWE-WebDev Bench as a community benchmark to enable such replication and help platform builders identify and address these gaps.   Code and benchmark resources are available at: https://github.com/snowmountainAi/webdevbench and https://webdevbench.com/.

ru.title: Оценка AI-ассистентов для разработки: от замысла к production-ready коду
ru.desc: В статье представлена комплексная система оценки SWE-WebDev Bench для проверки платформ, использующих AI для разработки веб-приложений. Фреймворк содержит 68 метрик, организованных по трем измерениям: тип взаимодействия, роль в разработке и уровень сложности проекта. Исследование выявило четыре основных проблемы текущего поколения AI-助手助手: сжатие требований в упрощенные планы, разрыв между интерфейсом и бэкендом, недостаточная готовность к production и критические уязвимости в безопасности. Авторы выпустили бенчмарк как открытый ресурс для сообщества разработчиков, чтобы помочь создателям платформ выявить и исправить выявленные проблемы.

en.title: SWE-WebDev Bench: Elevating AI App Development Evaluation
en.desc: The paper introduces SWE-WebDev Bench, a new evaluation framework designed to assess AI-driven application development platforms. It focuses on multiple aspects such as understanding requirements, making architectural decisions, ensuring code quality, and readiness for production. The framework includes 68 metrics organized into three dimensions: Interaction Mode, Agency Angle, and Complexity Tier, allowing for a comprehensive analysis of platforms. The evaluation of six platforms revealed significant shortcomings, including issues with specification, frontend-backend integration, production readiness, and security, highlighting the need for improvement in AI app builders.

zh.title: 评估AI应用开发平台的新标准
zh.desc: 本文提出了一种名为SWE-WebDev Bench的综合评估框架，用于评估AI驱动的应用开发平台。该框架涵盖多个维度，包括需求理解、架构决策、代码质量和生产准备。通过对六个平台的评估，发现当前AI应用构建者存在四个主要问题，如规范瓶颈和前后端解耦等。SWE-WebDev Bench旨在作为社区基准，帮助平台开发者识别和解决这些问题。

papers.title: APEX: Large-scale Multi-task Aesthetic-Informed Popularity Prediction for AI-Generated Music
papers.abstract: Abstract A large-scale multi-task learning framework for AI-generated music predicts both popularity and aesthetic quality using frozen audio embeddings from a self-supervised music understanding model, demonstrating strong generalization across different generative architectures.  AI-generated summary Music popularity prediction has attracted growing research interest, with relevance to artists, platforms, and recommendation systems. However, the explosive rise of AI-generated music platforms has created an entirely new and largely unexplored landscape, where a surge of songs is produced and consumed daily without the traditional markers of artist reputation or label backing. Key, yet unexplored in this pursuit is aesthetic quality. We propose APEX, the first large-scale multi-task learning framework for AI-generated music, trained on over 211k songs (10k hours of audio) from Suno and Udio, that jointly predicts engagement-based popularity signals - streams and likes scores - alongside five perceptual aesthetic quality dimensions from frozen audio embeddings extracted from MERT, a self-supervised music understanding model. Aesthetic quality and popularity capture complementary aspects of music that together prove valuable: in an out-of-distribution evaluation on the Music Arena dataset, comprising pairwise human preference battles across eleven generative music systems unseen during training, including aesthetic features consistently improves preference prediction, demonstrating strong generalisation of the learned representations across generative architectures.
affiliations: Singapore University of Technology and Design

ru.title: Эстетика и популярность: предсказание качества AI-музыки через мультизадачное обучение
ru.desc: В работе предложена APEX — первая крупномасштабная система мультизадачного обучения для музыки, созданной нейросетями. Модель одновременно предсказывает популярность (количество прослушиваний и лайков) и эстетические качества музыки, используя замороженные эмбеддинги из самообучаемой модели MERT. Система обучена на более чем 211 тысячах композициях (10 тысячах часов аудио) с платформ Suno и Udio. При оценке на неизвестных архитектурах моделей эстетические признаки значительно улучшают предсказание предпочтений слушателей, демонстрируя хорошую генерализацию подученных представлений.

en.title: APEX: Bridging Popularity and Aesthetic Quality in AI Music
en.desc: This paper introduces APEX, a multi-task learning framework designed to predict both the popularity and aesthetic quality of AI-generated music. It utilizes frozen audio embeddings from a self-supervised model to analyze over 211,000 songs, focusing on engagement metrics like streams and likes, as well as five dimensions of aesthetic quality. The framework demonstrates strong generalization across various generative music systems, showing that understanding aesthetic features can enhance predictions of listener preferences. This approach addresses the challenges posed by the rapid growth of AI-generated music, providing valuable insights for artists and recommendation systems.

zh.title: APEX：AI生成音乐的流行与美学双重预测
zh.desc: 这篇论文提出了一个名为APEX的大规模多任务学习框架，用于预测AI生成音乐的流行度和美学质量。该框架利用自监督音乐理解模型MERT提取的音频嵌入，分析了超过21.1万首歌曲的数据。研究表明，美学质量和流行度是音乐的重要互补方面，能够有效提升推荐系统的表现。通过在未见过的生成音乐系统上进行评估，验证了所学表示的强泛化能力。

papers.title: Diffusion Model as a Generalist Segmentation Learner
papers.abstract: Abstract Pretrained diffusion models can be adapted for semantic and open-vocabulary segmentation tasks through latent space conditioning and text-guided alignment, achieving state-of-the-art performance across diverse domains.  AI-generated summary Diffusion models are primarily trained for image synthesis, yet their denoising trajectories encode rich, spatially aligned visual priors. In this paper, we demonstrate that these priors can be utilized for text-conditioned semantic and open-vocabulary segmentation, and this approach can be generalized to various downstream tasks to make a general-purpose diffusion segmentation framework. Concretely, we introduce DiGSeg (Diffusion Models as a Generalist Segmentation Learner), which repurposes a pretrained diffusion model into a unified segmentation framework. Our approach encodes the input image and ground-truth mask into the latent space and concatenates them as conditioning signals for the diffusion U-Net. A parallel CLIP-aligned text pathway injects language features across multiple scales, enabling the model to align textual queries with evolving visual representations. This design transforms an off-the-shelf diffusion backbone into a universal interface that produces structured segmentation masks conditioned on both appearance and arbitrary text prompts. Extensive experiments demonstrate state-of-the-art performance on standard semantic segmentation benchmarks, as well as strong open-vocabulary generalization and cross-domain transfer to medical, remote sensing, and agricultural scenarios-without domain-specific architectural customization. These results indicate that modern diffusion backbones can serve as generalist segmentation learners rather than pure generators, narrowing the gap between visual generation and visual understanding.

ru.title: Диффузионные модели как универсальные сегментаторы с текстовой навигацией
ru.desc: Исследование показывает, что предобученные диффузионные модели можно эффективно адаптировать для задач семантической и открытослужебной сегментации изображений. Авторы предлагают DiGSeg — фреймворк, который переназначает диффузионную модель для единой архитектуры сегментации, кодируя входное изображение и маску в латентное пространство и добавляя текстовые сигналы через выравненный с CLIP путь. Подход объединяет визуальные представления с языковыми признаками на нескольких уровнях абстракции, позволяя модели работать с произвольными текстовыми запросами. Метод демонстрирует передовые результаты на бенчмарках семантической сегментации и отличную обобщаемость на медицинские изображения, спутниковые снимки и агрокультурные данные без специальной настройки архитектуры.

en.title: Transforming Diffusion Models into Powerful Segmentation Learners
en.desc: This paper presents DiGSeg, a novel framework that adapts pretrained diffusion models for semantic and open-vocabulary segmentation tasks. By leveraging the rich visual priors encoded in the denoising process of diffusion models, the authors create a unified segmentation system that integrates both image and text inputs. The model uses latent space conditioning and a text pathway to align visual features with textual queries, allowing for flexible and accurate segmentation. The results show that DiGSeg achieves state-of-the-art performance across various domains, demonstrating the potential of diffusion models as versatile tools for visual understanding.

zh.title: 扩散模型：通用分割学习的新视角
zh.desc: 本论文提出了一种新的方法，利用预训练的扩散模型进行语义和开放词汇分割任务。我们引入了DiGSeg框架，将扩散模型转变为统一的分割学习器，通过潜在空间条件和文本引导对齐来实现。该方法能够将输入图像和真实掩码编码为潜在空间，并结合文本特征，生成结构化的分割掩码。实验结果表明，该方法在多个标准语义分割基准上表现出色，并在医疗、遥感和农业等领域实现了强大的跨域迁移能力。

papers.title: MedSkillAudit: A Domain-Specific Audit Framework for Medical Research Agent Skills
papers.abstract: Abstract A domain-specific audit framework for medical research agent skills demonstrates reliable assessment consistency compared to expert review, supporting governance of specialized AI capabilities in healthcare applications.  AI-generated summary Background: Agent skills are increasingly deployed as modular, reusable capability units in AI agent systems. Medical research agent skills require safeguards beyond general-purpose evaluation, including scientific integrity, methodological validity, reproducibility, and boundary safety. This study developed and preliminarily evaluated a domain-specific audit framework for medical research agent skills, with a focus on reliability against expert review. Methods: We developed MedSkillAudit (skill-auditor@1.0), a layered framework assessing skill release readiness before deployment. We evaluated 75 skills across five medical research categories (15 per category). Two experts independently assigned a quality score (0-100), an ordinal release disposition (Production Ready / Limited Release / Beta Only / Reject), and a high-risk failure flag. System-expert agreement was quantified using ICC(2,1) and linearly weighted Cohen's kappa, benchmarked against the human inter-rater baseline. Results: The mean consensus quality score was 72.4 (SD = 13.0); 57.3% of skills fell below the Limited Release threshold. MedSkillAudit achieved ICC(2,1) = 0.449 (95% CI: 0.250-0.610), exceeding the human inter-rater ICC of 0.300. System-consensus score divergence (SD = 9.5) was smaller than inter-expert divergence (SD = 12.4), with no directional bias (Wilcoxon p = 0.613). Protocol Design showed the strongest category-level agreement (ICC = 0.551); Academic Writing showed a negative ICC (-0.567), reflecting a structural rubric-expert mismatch. Conclusions: Domain-specific pre-deployment audit may provide a practical foundation for governing medical research agent skills, complementing general-purpose quality checks with structured audit workflows tailored to scientific use cases.
affiliations: Department of Pathology, Zhongshan Hospital, Fudan University, Shanghai, China

ru.title: Автоматическая сертификация медицинских AI-навыков на уровне экспертов
ru.desc: В статье описывается MedSkillAudit — специализированная система оценки для проверки компонентов медицинских исследовательских агентов перед развертыванием. Система использует многоуровневый фреймворк для оценки научной целостности, методологической валидности и безопасности модульных навыков AI. Результаты показывают, что система достигает согласованности с экспертными оценками на уровне ICC(2,1) = 0,449, превышая межэкспертное согласие (ICC = 0,300), что демонстрирует надежность подхода. Исследование подтверждает, что предварительный предеплойментный аудит может служить практической основой для управления специализированными AI-возможностями в здравоохранении.

en.title: Ensuring Quality in AI: The MedSkillAudit Framework for Medical Research Skills
en.desc: This paper presents a specialized audit framework called MedSkillAudit for evaluating medical research agent skills in AI systems. The framework aims to ensure that these skills meet specific standards of scientific integrity and methodological validity before they are deployed. The study found that MedSkillAudit demonstrated a higher reliability in assessment compared to expert reviews, indicating its effectiveness in maintaining quality control. Overall, the framework provides a structured approach to govern AI capabilities in healthcare, enhancing the safety and reliability of medical research applications.

zh.title: 医疗研究代理技能的审计框架提升评估一致性
zh.desc: 本研究提出了一种针对医疗研究代理技能的特定领域审计框架，旨在提高评估的一致性。该框架名为MedSkillAudit，能够在技能部署前评估其准备情况。研究表明，MedSkillAudit的评估结果与专家评审相比具有更高的可靠性，尤其在科学完整性和方法有效性方面。通过对75项技能的评估，发现57.3%的技能未达到有限发布的标准，显示出该框架在医疗AI应用中的重要性。

papers.title: The First Token Knows: Single-Decode Confidence for Hallucination Detection
papers.abstract: Abstract First-token confidence (phi_first) derived from initial token distribution matches or exceeds semantic self-consistency in detecting hallucinations while being more computationally efficient.  AI-generated summary Self-consistency detects hallucinations by generating multiple sampled answers to a question and measuring agreement, but this requires repeated decoding and can be sensitive to lexical variation. Semantic self-consistency improves this by clustering sampled answers by meaning using natural language inference, but it adds both sampling cost and external inference overhead. We show that first-token confidence, phi_first, computed from the normalized entropy of the top-K logits at the first content-bearing answer token of a single greedy decode, matches or modestly exceeds semantic self-consistency on closed-book short-answer factual question answering. Across three 7-8B instruction-tuned models and two benchmarks, phi_first achieves a mean AUROC of 0.820, compared with 0.793 for semantic agreement and 0.791 for standard surface-form self-consistency. A subsumption test shows that phi_first is moderately to strongly correlated with semantic agreement, and combining the two signals yields only a small AUROC improvement over phi_first alone. These results suggest that much of the uncertainty information captured by multi-sample agreement is already available in the model's initial token distribution. We argue that phi_first should be reported as a default low-cost baseline before invoking sampling-based uncertainty estimation.

ru.title: Первый токен раскрывает истину: эффективное обнаружение галлюцинаций без повторной выборки
ru.desc: В этой работе предложен новый метод обнаружения галлюцинаций в LLM, основанный на анализе распределения вероятностей первого токена ответа. Авторы показывают, что мера уверенности phi_first, вычисленная из энтропии топ-K логитов на первом информативном токене, эффективнее существующих методов с точки зрения вычислительных затрат. Метод достигает метрики AUROC 0.820 на задаче факт-вопрос и превосходит семантическую самосогласованность, которая требует многократного декодирования и внешних моделей вывода. Исследование демонстрирует, что большая часть информации о неопределённости модели уже содержится в начальном распределении токенов, поэтому phi_first рекомендуется использовать как базовый метод перед применением более сложных подходов.

en.title: First-Token Confidence: A Smart and Efficient Way to Detect AI Hallucinations
en.desc: This paper introduces a new method called first-token confidence (phi_first) for detecting hallucinations in AI-generated responses. Unlike traditional self-consistency methods that require multiple samples and can be computationally expensive, phi_first uses the initial token distribution from a single greedy decode to assess confidence. The authors demonstrate that phi_first performs comparably or better than existing methods in terms of accuracy while being more efficient. They recommend using phi_first as a standard baseline for uncertainty estimation in machine learning models.

zh.title: 首个令牌置信度：高效检测幻觉的新方法
zh.desc: 本文提出了一种新的方法，称为首个令牌置信度（phi_first），用于检测AI生成内容中的幻觉。与传统的自一致性方法相比，phi_first在计算效率上更高，同时在检测准确性上表现相当或更好。通过分析模型在生成答案时的初始令牌分布，phi_first能够有效捕捉不确定性信息。研究结果表明，phi_first可以作为一种低成本的基线方法，在进行基于采样的不确定性估计之前使用。

papers.title: When to Think, When to Speak: Learning Disclosure Policies for LLM Reasoning
papers.abstract: Abstract Side-by-Side Interleaved Reasoning enables controlled disclosure timing in autoregressive models, improving accuracy and efficiency through interleaved private reasoning and delayed content release.  AI-generated summary In single-stream autoregressive interfaces, the same tokens both update the model state and constitute an irreversible public commitment. This coupling creates a silence tax: additional deliberation postpones the first task-relevant content, while naive early streaming risks premature commitments that bias subsequent generations. We introduce Side-by-Side (SxS) Interleaved Reasoning, which makes disclosure timing a controllable decision within standard autoregressive generation. SxS interleaves partial disclosures with continued private reasoning in the same context, but releases content only when it is supported by the reasoning so far. To learn such pacing without incentivizing filler, we construct entailment-aligned interleaved trajectories by matching answer prefixes to supporting reasoning prefixes, then train with SFT to acquire the dual-action semantics and RL to recover reasoning performance under the new format. Across two Qwen3 architectures/scales (MoE Qwen3-30B-A3B, dense Qwen3-4B) and both in-domain (AIME25) and out-of-domain (GPQA-Diamond) benchmarks, SxS improves accuracy--content-latency Pareto trade-offs under token-level proxies such as inter-update waiting.

ru.title: Контролируемое раскрытие мыслей: рассуждение без молчания
ru.desc: Статья представляет метод Side-by-Side Interleaved Reasoning для автарегрессивных моделей, который позволяет контролировать время раскрытия информации. Основная проблема заключается в том, что при однопотоковой генерации одинаковые токены одновременно обновляют состояние модели и представляют неотменяемое публичное обязательство, что приводит к компромиссу между качеством и задержкой вывода. Авторы предлагают чередовать частичное раскрытие содержимого с продолжением скрытого рассуждения в одном контексте, выпуская информацию только когда её поддерживает логика вычислений. Для обучения такой тактике используется энтайлмент-выравненные интерпретируемые траектории, а также методы supervised fine-tuning и reinforcement learning.

en.title: Controlled Disclosure for Enhanced Reasoning in AI
en.desc: This paper presents a new method called Side-by-Side (SxS) Interleaved Reasoning for autoregressive models, which allows for better control over when information is disclosed. By interleaving private reasoning with partial content release, the model can delay sharing information until it is fully supported by the reasoning process. This approach addresses the issue of 'silence tax' where early commitments can lead to biased outputs. The authors demonstrate that SxS enhances both accuracy and efficiency in generating responses across different model architectures and benchmarks.

zh.title: 并行交错推理：提升自回归模型的准确性与效率
zh.desc: 本文提出了一种名为"并行交错推理"（SxS）的新方法，旨在改善自回归模型的准确性和效率。通过在同一上下文中交错部分信息披露和私密推理，SxS使得内容的发布时机可以被控制。该方法通过构建与推理前缀相匹配的推理轨迹，避免了过早承诺带来的偏差。实验结果表明，SxS在多个基准测试中显著提高了模型的准确性和内容延迟的平衡。

papers.title: CreativityBench: Evaluating Agent Creative Reasoning via Affordance-Based Tool Repurposing
papers.abstract: Abstract Large language models demonstrate limited creative problem-solving abilities when required to repurpose objects based on affordance reasoning, indicating a gap in current AI capabilities for novel tool usage.  AI-generated summary Recent advances in large language models have led to strong performance on reasoning and environment-interaction tasks, yet their ability for creative problem-solving remains underexplored. We study this capability through the lens of creative tool use, where a model repurposes available objects by reasoning about their affordances and attributes rather than relying on canonical usage. As a first step, we introduce CreativityBench, a benchmark for evaluating affordance-based creativity in LLMs. To this end, we build a large-scale affordance knowledge base (KB) with 4K entities and 150K+ affordance annotations, explicitly linking objects, parts, attributes, and actionable uses. Building on this KB, we generate 14K grounded tasks that require identifying non-obvious yet physically plausible solutions under constraints. Evaluations across 10 state-of-the-art LLMs, including closed and open-source models, show that models can often select a plausible object, but fail to identify the correct parts, their affordances, and the underlying physical mechanism needed to solve the task, leading to a significant drop in performance. Furthermore, improvements from model scaling quickly saturate, strong general reasoning does not reliably translate to creative affordance discovery, and common inference-time strategies such as Chain-of-Thought yield limited gains. These results suggest that creative tool use remains a major challenge for current models, and that CreativityBench provides a useful testbed for studying this missing dimension of intelligence, with potential implications for planning and reasoning modules in future agents.

ru.title: Творческое переназначение предметов: где буксуют современные LLM
ru.desc: В работе исследуется способность больших языковых моделей к творческому решению проблем через призму нестандартного использования инструментов. Авторы представляют CreativityBench — новый бенчмарк с базой знаний о affordances (функциональных возможностях) объектов, содержащей 4K сущностей и 150K+ аннотаций, которая позволяет оценивать способность моделей к рассуждению о переназначении предметов. Тестирование 10 современных LLM показывает, что модели могут выбрать подходящий объект, но затрудняются в определении его частей, их функциональных возможностей и физических механизмов решения задачи. Результаты демонстрируют, что творческое использование инструментов остаётся серьёзным вызовом для текущих моделей, и что масштабирование моделей не гарантирует улучшения в этой области.

en.title: Bridging the Gap in AI Creativity with CreativityBench
en.desc: This paper explores the limitations of large language models (LLMs) in creative problem-solving, particularly in repurposing objects based on their affordances. The authors introduce CreativityBench, a benchmark designed to evaluate the creativity of LLMs by assessing their ability to use objects in novel ways. They create a comprehensive knowledge base with thousands of entities and affordance annotations to generate tasks that challenge models to find non-obvious solutions. The findings reveal that while LLMs can often select plausible objects, they struggle with identifying the correct parts and their affordances, highlighting a significant gap in their creative reasoning capabilities.

zh.title: 创造性工具使用：当前AI的挑战
zh.desc: 大型语言模型在基于可供性推理的物体重新利用方面表现出有限的创造性问题解决能力，显示出当前人工智能在新工具使用方面的不足。我们通过创造性工具使用的视角研究这一能力，模型通过推理物体的可供性和属性来重新利用可用物体，而不是依赖于传统用法。为此，我们引入了CreativityBench，这是一个评估大型语言模型基于可供性创造力的基准，并构建了一个包含4K实体和150K+可供性注释的大规模知识库。评估结果表明，尽管模型能够选择出合理的物体，但在识别正确的部件及其可供性方面仍存在显著不足，表明创造性工具使用仍然是当前模型面临的重大挑战。

papers.title: KinDER: A Physical Reasoning Benchmark for Robot Learning and Planning
papers.abstract: Abstract KinDER is a benchmark for physical reasoning in robotics that includes procedurally generated environments and baselines spanning multiple learning paradigms to address kinematic and dynamic constraint challenges.  AI-generated summary Robotic systems that interact with the physical world must reason about kinematic and dynamic constraints imposed by their own embodiment, their environment, and the task at hand. We introduce KinDER, a benchmark for Kinematic and Dynamic Embodied Reasoning that targets physical reasoning challenges arising in robot learning and planning. KinDER comprises 25 procedurally generated environments, a Gymnasium-compatible Python library with parameterized skills and demonstrations, and a standardized evaluation suite with 13 implemented baselines spanning task and motion planning, imitation learning, reinforcement learning, and foundation-model-based approaches. The environments are designed to isolate five core physical reasoning challenges: basic spatial relations, nonprehensile multi-object manipulation, tool use, combinatorial geometric constraints, and dynamic constraints, disentangled from perception, language understanding, and application-specific complexity. Empirical evaluation shows that existing methods struggle to solve many of the environments, indicating substantial gaps in current approaches to physical reasoning. We additionally include real-to-sim-to-real experiments on a mobile manipulator to assess the correspondence between simulation and real-world physical interaction. KinDER is fully open-sourced and intended to enable systematic comparison across diverse paradigms for advancing physical reasoning in robotics. Website and code: https://prpl-group.com/kinder-site/

ru.title: Бенчмарк для систематической оценки физического рассуждения роботов
ru.desc: KinDER — это бенчмарк для оценки физического рассуждения в робототехнике, содержащий 25 процедурно генерируемых окружений и 13 базовых моделей, охватывающих обучение с подкреплением, обучение по демонстрациям и методы на основе больших языковых моделей. Бенчмарк изолирует пять ключевых задач физического рассуждения: пространственные отношения, манипуляцию несколькими объектами без захвата, использование инструментов, комбинаторные геометрические и динамические ограничения. Авторы показали, что существующие подходы испытывают серьёзные трудности при решении большинства задач, что указывает на значительные пробелы в современных методах физического рассуждения. Бенчмарк включает экспериментальную валидацию на реальном манипуляторе, позволяя сравнивать различные парадигмы обучения для развития физического рассуждения в робототехнике.

en.title: Advancing Robotic Physical Reasoning with KinDER
en.desc: KinDER is a new benchmark designed to improve physical reasoning in robotics by providing a variety of procedurally generated environments. It focuses on kinematic and dynamic constraints that robots face when interacting with the physical world. The benchmark includes a Python library compatible with Gymnasium, offering parameterized skills and demonstrations, along with a standardized evaluation suite featuring 13 different learning approaches. By isolating key physical reasoning challenges, KinDER aims to highlight the limitations of current methods and facilitate systematic comparisons to enhance robotic learning and planning.

zh.title: KinDER：机器人物理推理的新基准
zh.desc: KinDER是一个针对机器人物理推理的基准测试，旨在解决运动学和动态约束的挑战。它包含25个程序生成的环境和一个兼容Gymnasium的Python库，提供参数化技能和示范。KinDER的评估套件包括13个基线，涵盖任务与运动规划、模仿学习、强化学习等多种学习范式。通过实证评估，现有方法在许多环境中表现不佳，显示出物理推理领域的显著差距。

papers.title: TT4D: A Pipeline and Dataset for Table Tennis 4D Reconstruction From Monocular Videos
papers.abstract: Abstract A novel reconstruction pipeline for table tennis gameplay from monocular broadcast videos achieves high-fidelity 3D ball trajectories and spin estimation through a learned lifting network that operates on unsegmented 2D tracks before time segmentation.  AI-generated summary We present TT4D, a large-scale, high-fidelity table tennis dataset. It provides 140+ hours of reconstructed singles and doubles gameplay from monocular broadcast videos, featuring multimodal annotations like high-quality camera calibrations, precise 3D ball positions, ball spin, time segmentation, and 3D human meshes over time. This rich data provides a new foundation for virtual replay, in-depth player analysis, and robot learning. The dataset's combination of scale and precision is achieved through a novel reconstruction pipeline. Prior methods first partition a game sequence into individual shot segments based on the 2D ball track, and only then attempt reconstruction. However, 2D-based time segmentation collapses under occlusion and varied camera viewpoints, preventing reliable reconstruction. We invert this paradigm by first lifting the entire unsegmented 2D ball track to 3D through a learned lifting network. This 3D trajectory then allows us to reliably perform time segmentation. The learned lifting network also infers the ball's spin, handles unreliable ball detections, and successfully reconstructs the ball trajectory in cases of high occlusion. This lift-first design is necessary, as our pipeline is the only method capable of reconstructing table tennis gameplay from general-view broadcast monocular videos. We demonstrate the dataset's fidelity through two downstream tasks: estimating the racket's pose \& velocity at impact, and training a generative model of competitive rallies.

ru.title: Поднять сначала, сегментировать потом: восстановление настольного тенниса через сеть лифтинга
ru.desc: Авторы представляют TT4D — крупный датасет с 140+ часами видео настольного тенниса из трансляций с точными 3D-аннотациями: траектории мяча, вращение, сегментация времени и 3D-модели игроков. Ключевое нововведение — инвертированный конвейр реконструкции: вместо сначала сегментировать видео по 2D-трекам мяча, сеть сначала восстанавливает полную 3D-траекторию из несегментированных 2D-данных, а потом выполняет временную сегментацию. Обученная сеть лифтинга справляется с окклюзией, оценивает вращение мяча и корректирует ошибки детектирования, что позволяет реконструировать игру из монокулярных видео общего вида. Датасет демонстрирует высокую точность на двух задачах: оценка позы и скорости ракетки в момент удара и обучение генеративной модели соревновательных розыгрышей.

en.title: Revolutionizing Table Tennis Analysis with 3D Reconstruction
en.desc: This paper introduces a new method for reconstructing table tennis gameplay from single-camera broadcast videos, achieving accurate 3D ball trajectories and spin estimation. The approach utilizes a learned lifting network that processes unsegmented 2D ball tracks before segmenting the video by time, overcoming challenges like occlusion and varying camera angles. The resulting dataset, TT4D, includes extensive gameplay footage with detailed annotations, enabling advanced analysis and applications in virtual replays and robotics. This innovative pipeline sets a new standard for reconstructing sports gameplay from monocular video sources.

zh.title: 乒乓球比赛重建的新视角
zh.desc: 本文提出了一种新颖的乒乓球比赛重建流程，能够从单目广播视频中高保真地重建3D球轨迹和旋转估计。该方法使用了一种学习提升网络，首先将未分段的2D轨迹提升到3D，然后再进行时间分段，从而克服了传统方法在遮挡和不同视角下的局限性。我们构建了一个名为TT4D的大规模高保真乒乓球数据集，包含140多个小时的重建比赛数据，提供了多模态注释，如高质量相机标定和精确的3D球位置。该数据集为虚拟重播、深入的球员分析和机器人学习提供了新的基础。
